summary: EditCol V2 — first iteration. Comprehensive backend pytest suite (26 tests covering auth/OTP/login/forgot-reset/editor onboarding/listing/projects/conversations/messages/WebSocket/reviews/reports/admin actions/brute-force/banned-user). All 26 backend tests PASS. Frontend Playwright walkthrough covered home, 5 legal pages, browse, admin login → console with 6 tabs, register → OTP step 2 with dev codes panel, OTP verify, and unverified dashboard banner — all pass. No critical issues found.

issues: Editor avatar images appear broken (alt text shows) because backend tests injected stub base64 strings like 'data:image/png;base64,iVBOR'. This is test-seed data — not a real product bug, but UI could add an avatar fallback (initials) when base64 fails to render.

action_items: (Optional) Add avatar fallback (initials / placeholder SVG) in EditorCard so corrupt/incomplete base64 doesn't show broken-image icon.
(Optional) Consider hiding the dev OTP panel when NODE_ENV=production (currently always shown after register).

(Optional) Login route currently relies on /api/auth/me 401 to detect logged-out state which is correct, but logs noisy 401 in console on every public page; consider a /api/auth/session check that returns 200 with {authenticated:false}.
Note: Backend tests left 2 public editors and projects in editcol_v2 DB. If a truly empty DB is required for demos, run a cleanup or drop the test data.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py recompute_trust includes 'verified' badge unconditionally on every recompute — overrides the eligibility-based logic in PUT /editors/me. This is fine functionally but should be conditional on the editor still being eligible.
WebSocket auth uses 60-min JWT; token transmitted as query param — acceptable but consider Origin check.
set_auth_cookies uses secure=False — OK for current preview (HTTPS via ingress) but should be True in production.

CORS allow_origins='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is technically a spec violation (browsers reject *) — currently works because requests are same-origin via K8s ingress; tighten origins for production.
Forgot-password returns 200 for non-existent users (good — prevents enumeration), but reset_token_dev is only included for real users — leaks existence indirectly. Acceptable in dev mode.

verify-otp uses find_one without sorting — if multiple unused OTPs exist (e.g., resend), behaviour depends on Mongo natural order. Sort by created_at desc, or invalidate prior on resend (already done — good).
test_credentials: Admin financeabuzar@gmail.com / EditColAdmin@2026!Secure — works. Test users created per-run with random suffix.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created 2 editors (public), 1 project, 1 conversation, 1 review, 1 report (resolved/banned), 1 brute-force test user. Frontend tests created 2 additional client users via /register.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite at /app/backend/tests/test_editcol.py is full regression. Uses websockets pkg (already installed). All 26 tests stateful, must run in collection order. Brute-force lockout is keyed by IP+email, so re-running tests works because identifier varies per email. WebSocket test connects via wss://<preview>/api/ws?token=... — confirmed works through K8s ingress. DB now contains seed data — drop editcol_v2 collections (users/editors/projects/conversations/messages/reviews/reports/login_attempts/otp_codes/password_reset_tokens) before any 'empty DB' demos. Admin user is idempotently re-seeded on startup.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All 26 backend endpoints behave as specified; UI integration verified for home, legal (5 routes), browse, login→admin, register→OTP→verify, and unverified-dashboard banner. The only test-script fix needed was lowercasing the test email constants because backend normalises emails to lowercase (correct behaviour).
